English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
David Lyons and Matthew Lyons. Cornell divestment movement collection
Lyons, David, 1935-
Lyons, Matthew Nemiroff.
18-2-2541
1 cubic feet. 1 cubic feet.
1976-1987. Reports, legal documents, memos, articles, leaflets, posters, and other publications. Reports; legal documents; memos; and articles, leaflets, posters, and other publications, all relating to the movement for Cornell divestment in South Africa. Collection material in English 2093107
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, June 2003
Date modified:
Anne Jones, March 2012
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Matthew Lyons (Cornell University Class of 1986) helped to organize and participated in the first divestment sit-ins at Cornell's Day Hall in April-May 1985, as well as the May 8, 1985 action, "Take It To the Straight." In the fall of 1985, he helped to coordinate the divestment movement's daily sit-ins and civil disobedience at Day Hall. David Lyons, Matthew's father, is professor of law and philosophy at Cornell University. He was part of the first group of Cornell faculty and staff to be arrested in the divestment sit-ins in April 1985. He helped to draft the principal Faculty and Staff Against Apartheid (FSAA) documents including "Why Cornell Should Divest" and the FSAA's reply to the Proxy Review Committee Report on divestment. He represented Shantytown residents within the Cornell judicial system with regard to their complaints against the central administration.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Reports; legal documents; memos; and articles, leaflets, posters, and other publications, all relating to the movement for Cornell divestment in South Africa.
Cite As:
David Lyons and Matthew Lyons Cornell divestment movement collection, #18-2-2541. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
South Africa -- Economic conditions -- 1961- United States -- Foreign relations -- South Africa. Disinvestment. Students -- Political activity. Student movements. Cornell University -- : Students. Cornell University -- : Faculty.
